summary.intent: Implement CHA-19: a CRM tab Web UI for the Stokowski project, wiring up the existing CRM backend (contacts, matters, interactions) to a new Preact SPA page with navigation, i18n, and nav count badge.
summary.outcome: All 9 planned files were implemented (4 new, 5 modified), quality gates passed (cargo check, biome, tests), and the work was committed. A 10th file (page-settings.js) was also modified to add the CRM nav link to the settings sidebar.

repo: The entire frontend uses WebSocket RPC via sendRpc() for all CRUD operations — zero pages use REST. The CEO review's recommendation to add ~15 REST handlers was incorrect; engineering review corrected this to reuse existing RPC methods.

All pages follow the page-channels.js pattern: single large file (~1500-2000 lines) with internal Preact components, registerPage()/registerPrefix() for routing, signal() for state, and sendRpc() for data access.
Feature gating uses crm_enabled: bool in GonData (Rust-injected JSON), matching the graphql_enabled and voice_enabled patterns. JS checks gon.get('crm_enabled') before registering routes.
NavCounts are built via a tokio::join! block in build_nav_counts() in templates.rs. Adding CRM count adds ~1ms latency and currently loads full contact list to get count (len() call).
Navigation for top-level pages (CRM, projects, monitoring) uses registerPrefix() not registerPage(), supporting both /crm and /crm/:contactId sub-routes.
The settings sidebar (page-settings.js) controls visibility of top-level nav links — CRM had to be added to getSectionItems, getVisibleSections filter, and pageSectionHandlers.
i18n uses namespace loader pattern in i18n.js — each page registers its own namespace (e.g., 'crm') pointing to a locales/{lang}/crm.js file.
PII fields (email, phone) are Secret<String> in Rust but LiveCrmService.contact_to_json() already calls expose_secret() for authenticated JSON serialization — no additional exposure needed.
SQLite CRM store's list_filtered() does NOT escape % and _ wildcards in LIKE queries — searching for % matches all contacts. Low severity in single-tenant context.
RPC methods are registered at gateway/src/methods/services.rs:4516 — 14 CRM methods covering contacts, channels, matters, and interactions CRUD.

code.finding: All 14 CRM RPC method registrations live here: crm.contacts.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.channels.list/upsert/delete, crm.matters.list/get/upsert/delete, crm.interactions.list/record/delete
SpaRoutes and NavCounts structs live here; crm_enabled bool and crm NavCount were added matching graphql_enabled pattern
Controls top-level nav visibility — getSectionItems, getVisibleSections, and pageSectionHandlers all needed CRM entries for the tab to be reachable

workflow: Headless orchestration agents must resolve all open architecture decisions autonomously — the eng review resolved 5 open decisions (RPC vs REST, route depth, feature gating, single file, PII display) without human input.
Running cargo check and biome separately is necessary — the project has no unified 'check all' script.

The CEO review stage can produce incorrect technical recommendations (e.g., REST endpoints when RPC already exists); an engineering review pass is essential for scope correction before implementation.
Background task outputs (TaskOutput tool calls) required polling to retrieve results — multiple TaskOutput calls were needed before results were available.

friction: Multiple rounds of exploration were needed to locate where the CRM nav link needed to be wired — page-settings.js was not in the original 9-file scope but turned out to be required for navigation to work.
Background cargo check and test runs required repeated TaskOutput polling before results were available, adding latency to quality verification.
No unified quality check script — had to run cargo check, biome, and test suites separately across multiple crates (crm, web, gateway).

The settings page nav architecture (getSectionItems + getVisibleSections + pageSectionHandlers) required reading large portions of page-settings.js multiple times to understand all three injection points.
open_items: E2E Playwright tests (crm.spec.js) — test spec was defined in the engineering review but implementation deferred to a separate ticket requiring E2E infra changes (gateway running with CRM feature enabled).
Optimistic locking for concurrent CRM updates — last-write-wins is acceptable for v1 single-tenant but should be tracked; requires updated_at comparison on upsert.
Contact count optimization — NavCounts currently calls list_contacts().len() loading all contacts; a count_contacts() SELECT COUNT(*) method should be added when contact counts exceed ~1000.
LIKE wildcard escaping in CRM search — % and _ in search terms are passed through unescaped, causing wider-than-intended matches. Low severity, single-tenant only.
French and Chinese translations — locale files (fr/crm.js, zh/crm.js) were created with English fallback strings; actual translations are a separate i18n pass.
Matter kanban drag-and-drop view — v1 uses table with status dropdown; Phase 2 feature.
Contact merge/dedup — detect and merge duplicate contacts across channels; Phase 2.
PII reveal-on-click masking — v1 shows email/phone directly; masking with reveal interaction was identified as a delight opportunity but deferred.
